scope: L3 Obstetrics & Gynaecology + Medicine quality audit
policy: L3-minimum: any question that cannot legitimately reach L3 is DISCARDED. Verdicts: keep | rewrite | discard.
failure_modes: bundled-correct-option: lead-in singular but correct answer lists multiple discrete actions
implausible-distractors: 3+ distractors so wrong rule-out solves it without reasoning
diagnosis-named: stem/lead-in names diagnosis when it could be withheld

verdicts.fix_sketch: Genuine L3: tests AU-specific 8-year Mirena contraception licence vs HMB 5-year licence vs MHT 5-year licence. Distractor D (indefinite) and E (switch to copper) are credible look-alikes; A and B are common misconceptions. Multi-step (recognise HMB resolved, recognise contraception indication, recall AU 2023 extension).

Tests 72h vs 120h EC windows + BMI fall-off + IUD-declined. Distractors are all real EC options; LNG-3mg-double-dose is an actual practiced off-label that has to be ruled out on window not dose. Multi-step.

Genuine L3: requires recognising pyridoxine failure + position on antiemetic ladder. Doxylamine-pyridoxine vs ondansetron vs steroid step-up is a real exam discrimination. Discharge is a clear safety distractor but the other three are credible.
AU-specific 250/625/1500 IU dosing - genuine 3-step (recognise indication + recall AU dose split at 12 wk + reject UK 1500 trap). Distractors all plausible misreads.
Prior GBS-affected infant means screen-irrelevant + universal IAP. Tests RANZCOG indication recall - non-trivial because 'screen anyway' is the immediate-instinct trap.

NCSP pathway for non-16/18 HPV + pHSIL on reflex -> direct colposcopy. Tests recognition of pHSIL as colposcopy indication. Distractors credible (12-month repeat is the standard non-16/18 with NILM trap).

EPDS 16 is above-threshold and item-10 zero is reassuring. Correct option B 'refer to GP within 1-2 weeks' is right but distractors C (ED today) and D (start sertraline today by nurse) and E (baby blues at 6 wk) are weak. Replace with: (a) refer for psychological therapy under Medicare MHTP without GP review; (b) telephone advice to GP same day; (c) repeat EPDS in 4 weeks to confirm. Keep E (baby-blues misreading) as it is the genuine confound.

Transdermal E + micronised progesterone is genuinely first-line in AU per AMS 2024; oral CEE and oral MPA (option A, B) are credible older choices, tibolone and vaginal-oestrogen-monotherapy are real but wrong for systemic VMS. Multi-step.

Correct option text reads 'Tubal ectopic in the RIGHT ampulla' but stem says LEFT-sided pain - factual inconsistency. Also 'most likely diagnosis' is recognition-style L3 but the correct-option diagnosis label gives away the answer if the student spots that ectopic is the only ectopic-named option. Rewrite: change lead-in to 'most appropriate next investigation' (TVUS + serial beta-hCG vs MTX vs laparoscopy) so it tests management not pattern-match. Fix laterality typo.

RCOG/ASUM diagnostic criteria for miscarriage (CRL>=7mm no FH, MSD>=25mm no embryo). Tests recall of CRL cutoff vs MSD cutoff vs ratio - all real ultrasound criteria. Genuine 3-option discrimination among A/B/E.

Stem essentially names pre-eclampsia + seizure = eclampsia; lead-in asks first-line drug. Single-step drug-for-condition recall (eclampsia -> MgSO4). Distractors are caricatures (immediate GA C/S without anticonvulsant; phenytoin; diazepam single 5mg). DISCARD per L3-minimum policy.

Painless bright red APH classic praevia recognition - single-step pattern match. Distractors all credible APH causes but vasa praevia + abruption + rupture are easily ruled out by 'painless + no contractions + previous fundal placenta migrated'. The pattern-recognition is single-concept. DISCARD or rewrite as management question (next investigation: TVUS placental localisation vs Kleihauer vs CTG + steroids).

Cord prolapse - elevate presenting part is correct first manoeuvre but distractors C (oxytocin in transverse lie at 3cm) and D (bladder fill only, no elevation) and E (terbutaline without elevation) are wildly wrong. Replace with closer alternatives: (a) elevate + tocolysis with terbutaline only (omits position change); (b) elevate + bladder fill only en route to theatre (legitimate alternative but less preferred); (c) all-fours position + manual elevation (correct alternative position). Keep A (cord replacement - real wrong-historical-teaching trap).

Term PROM with negative GBS - genuine choice between immediate IOL vs expectant up to 24h. Distractors A (96h discharge), C (immediate C/S), D (broad-spec abx with neg GBS), E (tocolysis at term) are caricatures but the question still rewards real RANZCOG ROM-at-term knowledge. Borderline; keep but acknowledge distractors could be tightened.

Empirical endometriosis therapy without laparoscopy is now first-line per RANZCOG/ESHRE 2022. Distractors A (laparoscopy first - the older paradigm), B (GnRH-agonist + add-back upfront), E (CA-125 for dx) are genuine traps; D (hysterectomy first-line) is a caricature. Multi-step.
2023 PCOS Guideline: oligo + hyperandrogenism = 2 of 3 Rotterdam criteria met, USS not needed for diagnosis in adults. Distractors all real exam traps (defer for USS, LH:FSH, ACTH stim, AMH-alone).

Lead-in primes PALM-COEIN and asks classification - so the question is essentially 'what was on her USS' but the USS in this stem was 18 months ago and unremarkable. The 44-year-old + heavy + regular pattern is non-specific; either L (fibroid) or A (adenomyosis) is defensible without current imaging. Rewrite to provide a current TVUS finding in data_table that distinguishes - or change lead-in to 'most appropriate next investigation' (TVUS vs endometrial sampling vs hysteroscopy).

Stem does not actually give the OGTT values - asks student to interpret thresholds without showing values. Either add OGTT values to data_table (fasting + 1h + 2h) or change lead-in to 'which is the correct ADIPS fasting threshold'. As written, option C is unanswerable from the stem.

Turtle sign + first manoeuvre = McRoberts + suprapubic. Single-step ALSO/ANZCOR drill recall. Distractors A (fundal pressure), C (Zavanelli first), D (symphysiotomy bedside), E (cut cord then C/S) are caricatures. Pattern is single-concept; DISCARD per L3-minimum policy. (Could be salvaged as second-line-after-McRoberts-fails L4.)

PID -> ceftriaxone + doxycycline + metronidazole regimen. Single-step Therapeutic-Guidelines recall. Distractors A (amox+metro), B (cipro-which is no longer used due to GC resistance), C (doxy mono), E (azithro mono) are all clearly inadequate cover. Pure regimen recall. DISCARD.

Multiple UKMEC-3/4 stacking (migraine-with-aura UKMEC 4 -> rules out CHC; postpartum + breastfeeding + BMI 33 + ankle ORIF -> DMPA caution; LNG-IUS UKMEC 1). Genuine multi-criterion synthesis. Distractors all real methods. POP also defensible - the LNG-IUS preference comes from long-acting reversible + heavy menstrual control.

Correct option bundles: fibrinolysis + ASA + clopidogrel + enoxaparin + transfer for PCI 3-24h. Lead-in 'single most appropriate immediate reperfusion strategy' is singular. The competing options also bundle (PCI + anticoag en route). Tighten by restating lead-in 'most appropriate reperfusion strategy' (drops 'single', acknowledges combination).

GRACE 158 + NSTE-ACS + recurrent ischaemia within 12h -> 24h invasive. Distractors A (24h - correct), B (2h-immediate), C (72h-delayed), D (selective), E (defer-CKD) are all real ESC/ANZ pathway tiers. Genuine multi-step (calculate-risk-tier + recall timing-cutoffs).

Bundling is congruent with 'plan' lead-in. Tests the 6-week DAPT minimum vs DES-recent-PCI principle - real ANZ guidance. Distractors credible (stop both, continue both, bridge with LMWH, proceed unchanged).

Apixaban 2.5 BD dose-reduction (age>=80, weight 56, Cr 138 - 3/3 criteria). Rivaroxaban distractor doesn't have age/wt dose-reduction. Aspirin clearly wrong with prior stroke. Multi-step (calculate criteria + recall threshold).
HFrEF + symptomatic AF on optimal HF therapy -> CASTLE-AF/RAFT-AF evidence for catheter ablation as preferred rhythm-control. Distractors all genuine candidate strategies. Multi-step.

Correct option bundles ARNI-switch + spiro + dapa over subsequent visits (three additions). Lead-in 'next addition or modification' is singular. Tighten lead-in to 'most appropriate medication strategy' OR pick single next step (most-impactful = ARNI switch first). Distractors are credible single moves - ivabradine, digoxin, frusemide up, CRT-D.

HFpEF + T2DM + obesity -> empagliflozin first-line (EMPEROR-Preserved). Distractors A (ARNI - PARAGON neutral), B (spiro - TOPCAT neutral), C (BB - no HFpEF benefit) are real real-world traps. Multi-step.

Lead-in 'next steps' is plural so bundling is congruent. Tests high-risk syncope features (bifascicular block + abrupt + brief jerks misinterpreted) plus AU driving regulations (Austroads). Distractors B vs A vary only on driving advice - clean discrimination.

Resistant HTN + hypokalaemia + metabolic alkalosis -> screen primary aldosteronism with ARR. Distractors all real secondary HTN work-up tests; A (repeat metanephrines) and E (empirical spironolactone) are real traps.
Grade 2 HTN after 12 wk lifestyle -> add ACE-i. NHFA-aligned. Distractor C (ABPM first) is a real trap when home-BP already confirms. E (full secondary HTN workup) overinvestigation trap.

Stem explicitly names Mobitz II AV block in correct option, and the constant-PR with intermittent dropped beat + symptomatic presyncope + RBBB QRS is textbook 'symptomatic Mobitz II -> PPM' single-step. Distractors B (atropine), C (iso then discharge), D (reduce perindopril), E (AV node ablation as definitive) are caricatures. DISCARD.

Rural STEMI with FMC-to-balloon 200 min > 120 min cutoff -> fibrinolysis + pharmaco-invasive transfer. Distractor A (transfer for primary PCI despite >120) is the real trap. Tests AU/ANZ STEMI pathway threshold.

Posterior STEMI as STEMI-equivalent (V7-9 elevation). Tests recognition that isolated ST depression V1-3 + tall R + post-leads elevation = STEMI activation. Distractor A (NSTEMI conservative path) is the genuine trap. Wellens / RBBB / digoxin distractors weaker but acceptable.

Correct option bundles 'ticagrelor + duration + then aspirin alone' - reasonable. Distractor D (prasugrel for 6 months) implies a duration that does not match current AU practice (DAPT 12 months for ACS). Replace D with: 'prasugrel for 12 months then aspirin alone' (real ticagrelor-vs-prasugrel call). Borderline keep.

Correct option bundles 3 actions (IV frusemide + GTN + NIV CPAP). Lead-in 'most appropriate initial management' singular but bundle congruent. Distractors B (fluid bolus + metoprolol for tachycardia - caricature), D (withhold ACE/BB + dobutamine - caricature), E (PO frusemide + metolazone in acute APO - caricature). Replace with closer alternatives: (a) IV frusemide alone without nitrate/NIV; (b) NIV + IV GTN without frusemide; (c) IV frusemide + NIV without GTN.

Correct option bundles 3 actions (add spiro + dapa + switch ARNI). Lead-in 'next step' is singular. Tighten lead-in to 'most appropriate medication strategy' OR isolate single best next move. Distractors B (digoxin for prognosis), D (CRT without further uptitration), E (switch to ARB + ivabradine) are real-world traps but the bundle vs each-single-move mismatch is the issue.

Correct option bundles 3 actions (apixaban + BB + early DCCV + ablation consideration). EAST-AFNET-4-aligned. Bundle is OK but the 'consider ablation' tail is vague. Trim correct to 'apixaban + BB + early DCCV' and let ablation be a separate downstream decision.

SVT in asthmatic with adenosine relative caution (bronchospasm risk). Verapamil is correct in stable narrow-complex SVT in adult non-WPW. Distractor A (adenosine despite asthma) is the discriminator - genuine ANZCOR/RACGP awareness. Multi-step.
COPD GOLD E (>=2 exac, mMRC 2, eos low) -> LAMA/LABA. Distractors A (LAMA alone), C (LABA/ICS for COPD - low eos = no benefit), D (triple before LAMA/LABA), E (theophylline) are all real. Multi-step.

Type-2 resp failure from over-oxygenation in COPD exac + features of CO2 narcosis (flap, drowsy) -> titrate O2 + BiPAP. Distractor B (intubation), E (HFNC instead of BiPAP) are real exam traps. Multi-step.

Lead-in asks 'classification + corresponding initial treatment' (explicit bundle). Acceptable. Distractor C (life-threatening + immediate intubation) without trial of medical therapy is a caricature; E (severe + IV Mg first-line before salbutamol/steroid) is wrong ladder. Tighten E to real Aus Asthma Handbook ladder.
Uncontrolled mild asthma + planning pregnancy -> AIR with low-dose ICS-formoterol. Tests Aus Asthma Handbook 2024 AIR/MART terminology. Distractors all real ladder steps. Multi-step.

sPESI 0 + no RV strain + stable home -> outpatient apixaban. Lead-in essentially asks single strategy. Distractor B (warfarin bridge) is the older default trap. Multi-step (recognise low-risk PE + select DOAC over warfarin + outpatient eligibility).

Aspiration pneumonia in nursing-home resident with recent broad-spec abx (HCAP equivalent) -> pip-taz. Distractor C (PO amox monotherapy in confused hypoxic patient) and E (vanc + cipro empirically without MRO data) are caricatures. Replace with: (a) IV ceftriaxone + IV azithromycin (standard CAP but misses anaerobes/Pseudomonas); (b) IV benzylpenicillin + IV metronidazole (old-style 'aspiration' regimen without Pseudomonas cover); (c) IV meropenem + vancomycin (over-broad without MRSA/Pseudomonas risk data).

AF onset ambiguous (32 h current but milder episode 4 days ago) -> TOE-guided cardioversion + 4-wk post-CV anticoag regardless of CHA2DS2-VA. Distractor A (DCCV without anticoag because <48h) is the trap that overlooks the milder episode 4 days ago. Multi-step.

T2DM diagnosis with HbA1c unreliable in haemoglobinopathy -> repeat fasting glucose for confirmation. Tests RACGP red-book diagnostic algorithm + recognition that haemoglobinopathy invalidates HbA1c. Subtopic_detail field is corrupted ('RACGP)' only) - flag for metadata fix.

T2DM + HFrEF + CKD + albuminuria -> SGLT2i for cardiorenal indication regardless of HbA1c. Distractors all real T2DM second-line agents. Genuine multi-step (recognise compelling indications + reject HbA1c-only thinking).

T2DM + fasting hyperglyc + plateau on GLP-1 -> basal insulin start. Tests fasting-dominant pattern -> basal-only over basal-bolus or premix. Distractors all real insulin regimens; B (basal-bolus) and A (premix) are credible alternatives that overshoot complexity.

DKA + first 15 min -> fluid first. Single-step ANZCOR/eTG DKA algorithm recall. Distractors B (insulin without fluid), C (HCO3 bolus), D (KCl pre-insulin), E (SC long-acting) are all caricatures. DISCARD per L3-minimum policy.

HHS vs DKA discrimination: osmolality>320 + ketones<3 + pH>7.30. Tests precise biochemical criteria. Distractor A (BGL alone), C (AKI), D (precipitant), E (GCS) are all features present in both - discrimination must be biochemical. Multi-step.

Trimethoprim + gliclazide interaction + CKD reduces clearance. Real AMH/AusDI interaction trap. Distractors A (blame basal), C (metformin causing hypo - caricature), D (cefalexin causing hypo - false), E (glucagon dose) all real-world misattributions. Multi-step.

Thyroid storm - stem essentially names it via Burch-Wartofsky-like features. Correct option bundles 6 components. Lead-in 'initial medical management' singular but bundle congruent. Distractor B (amiodarone - contraindicated, real trap), C (sepsis), D (myxoedema coma - opposite dx), E (single carbimazole + discharge) - mostly caricatures. Tighten by lead-in 'most appropriate medical strategy' and replacing D and E with closer wrong-choices: (a) PTU monotherapy without iodine/BB; (b) Lugol's iodine before PTU (sequence error).

Subclinical hypothyroidism + TPO+ + preconception -> treat to TSH<2.5. Tests ATA preconception threshold. Distractor B (no treatment) is the watch-and-wait trap that misses pregnancy goal. Multi-step.

Correct option bundles 3 actions (IV Ca gluconate + Mg replacement + PO calcitriol). Symptomatic hypocalcaemia + QTc 510 indication for IV Ca. Distractor C (CaCl peripheral - sclerosant trap), D (Mg only without Ca - misses primary problem), E (cinacalcet - wrong direction) are caricatures. Replace one with closer alternative: 'PO calcium carbonate alone with cardiology review for QTc' (under-treats with symptoms).

Severe hyperkalaemia + ECG changes -> Ca gluconate first. Single-step ANZCOR/eTG hyperkalaemia algorithm. Distractors B-E all individually correct downstream steps just sequenced wrong. Pure sequencing recall. DISCARD per L3-minimum policy. (Could be salvaged as L4 'after Ca, what next'.)

TIA -> same-day CT/CTA + aspirin load + stroke unit. Bundled correct option (3 actions). Lead-in 'combination of management steps' is plural so congruent. Distractor B (ABCD2-only - outdated), C (outpatient 2 wk - wrong), D (empirical anticoag for presumed AF - wrong), E (Doppler in 4 wk - wrong). Acceptable but tighten distractors as they currently caricature each tier.

First-seizure workup (MRI+EEG) plus Austroads private 6mo no-drive. Lead-in 'combination of investigation and driving advice' is plural so bundling OK. Distractor B (start AED + drive in 1 mo - 2 wrongs), C (LP+ceftriaxone for meningitis - no meningism), D (admit VEEG today - wrong tier), E (cleared commercial driving - wrong). All caricatures. Replace 2 distractors: (a) MRI/EEG outpatient + no driving for 3 months (wrong duration); (b) MRI/EEG + drive immediately as single-seizure not epilepsy (wrong threshold).

Refractory status (2 benzo doses, 25 min) -> 2nd-line AED (levetiracetam per ESETT). Distractor A (third benzo - wrong, beyond max), C (RSI without AED), D (NG phenytoin - wrong route), E (Wernicke prevention - not the priority). Multi-step (recognise refractory definition + select AED).

GCA + visual symptoms + RAPD -> IV methylprednisolone, biopsy, dual specialty. Bundle has 4 actions. The PO-pred-only distractor (B) is the key discriminator (visual involvement upgrades to IV pulse). Acceptable bundle but lead-in singular - tighten to 'most appropriate combination of next steps'. Otherwise OK.

Thunderclap headache + normal CT at 14h -> LP for xanthochromia. Single-step algorithm recall (CT<6h sensitivity falls). Distractors B (discharge), D (empirical meningitis cover), E (MRI gad - wrong test) caricatures. Only C (CTA upfront) is a real alternative but tested as a single fact. DISCARD per L3-minimum policy.

Refractory anaphylaxis (2 IM doses fail) -> IV adrenaline infusion + fluids + airway prep. Bundle 3 actions. Distractor B (continue IM only forever - caricature), C (steroid + antihistamine alone - misses refractory definition), D (glucagon - reserved for beta-blocker overdose), E (neb adrenaline + salbutamol without IV escalation). Replace with closer alternatives: (a) repeat IM + start IV fluids without IV adrenaline (under-treatment); (b) IV adrenaline bolus rather than infusion (real anti-pattern); (c) call MET + continue IM as bridge (delays definitive).

Postmenopausal IDA + FOBT+ -> bidirectional endoscopy. Tests recognition that postmenopausal IDA mandates GI workup. Distractor C (CT colonography first-line - real alternative when colonoscopy unsuitable). Multi-step.

B12 deficiency with subacute combined degeneration on PPI + metformin background. Neurological features mandate parenteral B12 not oral. Distractor A (oral cyanocobalamin) is the trap; D (cease metformin and recheck) delays treatment. Multi-step.

Stem essentially gives warm AIHA dx (DAT IgG+, spherocytes, SLE background). Correct option bundles steroid + cautious transfusion + folate. Lead-in 'combination of treatments' plural so OK. Distractor B (steroid + strict no-transfusion - factually wrong dogma), D (TTP-like plasma exchange - wrong dx), E (no steroid for 1 wk - wrong) are caricatures. Replace with closer real alternatives: (a) IVIG monotherapy without steroid (real but not first-line); (b) rituximab upfront (real second-line - tests sequencing).

Acute chest syndrome in SCD -> exchange transfusion target HbS <30%. Tests sickle-specific transfusion strategy. Distractor A (simple top-up - real-world trap, can worsen by raising viscosity without lowering HbS), B (anticoag for PE), D (continue current), E (hydroxyurea acutely - useless). Multi-step.
Wells unlikely + age-adjusted D-dimer 680 (cutoff age*10 = 740) -> exclude PE. Tests AGE-ADJUSTED cutoff vs fixed 500 cutoff trap. Distractor B (CTPA because >500) is the precise trap. Multi-step.

Cancer-associated VTE in metastatic CRC -> apixaban or LMWH preferred (Caravaggio/HOKUSAI-cancer). Riva avoided in GI-active cancer due to bleed risk. Distractor A (riva - real but higher bleed in GI cancers), C (warfarin - outdated), E (aspirin - inadequate) are real-world alternatives. Multi-step.

HIT + DVT + dual organ failure -> non-heparin alternative. Bivalirudin chosen for combined renal+hepatic impairment (eliminated via plasma proteases). Distractor B (fondaparinux off-label - real alternative when no acute thrombosis but here HITT confirmed makes it controversial), C (argatroban standard dose - hepatic impairment requires dose adjustment, so 'standard' makes it wrong), D (warfarin straight - well-known venous-limb-gangrene trap), E (stop heparin observe - inadequate). Acceptable but D too easy.
Severe ITP with active bleeding + plt 4 -> combination IV methylpred + IVIG (ICIS 2019 guideline). Distractor B (PO pred alone) is the real trap (slower response when bleeding). Multi-step.

Suspected APML -> urgent transfer + empirical ATRA. Bundles transfer + bone marrow + ATRA. Distractor A (outpatient 2 wk), B (broad-spec abx + observe), C (transfuse only), E (repeat FBC 48h) caricatures. Lead-in 'single most appropriate next step' is singular but correct option is multi-action. Tighten lead-in to 'most appropriate disposition' or split into 'next step' (ATRA empirically).

CML chronic phase -> imatinib first-line. Single-step TKI recall. Distractors B/C (other TKIs) and D (ponatinib first-line - wrong) and E (HU as definitive - wrong) caricatures. The lithium and CHF background are red herrings that should drive choice. Discard or rewrite to leverage the pleural-effusion-caution (dasatinib trap) and CHF as the real discriminator.

Richter transformation suspicion: SUVmax>10 + LDH rise + B-sx. Tests PET-guided biopsy thresholds. Distractor A/B/D/E are individual findings that don't independently indicate transformation. Multi-step.

Young woman + cHL + fertility preservation BEFORE chemo. Tests recognition that fertility counselling happens upfront. Distractor B (defer to after cycle 1 - real trap because urgent, but fertility services can usually be done in <2 weeks), C (BEACOPP first-line - older trap), D (RT only - wrong), E (repeat biopsy - wrong). Multi-step.

MM with cord-compression risk + AKI + hypercalcaemia. Correct option bundles 4 actions. Lead-in 'combination of next steps' plural so OK. Distractors B (workup before tx - misses urgency), C (dex + outpatient - inadequate), D (lenalidomide without renal-dose adjust), E (HD to clear paraprotein - wrong) caricatures. Tighten distractors.

TLS high-risk (Burkitt + LDH >10x ULN + baseline urate 0.62) -> rasburicase + aggressive hydration. Distractor B (allopurinol only - inadequate for high-risk), C (urinary alkalinisation - outdated, increases Ca-PO4 precipitation), D (delay chemo - unsafe in Burkitt), E (upfront HD - over-treatment). Multi-step.

Febrile neutropenia + high-risk MASCC -> empiric pip-taz monotherapy per eTG. Distractor A (PO outpatient) over-relies on low-risk MASCC (this is high-risk), C (triple from outset before culture data), D (ceftriaxone - inadequate Pseudomonas cover), B (meropenem - too broad first-line in stable patient). Multi-step.

Malignant cord compression -> high-dose dex + urgent MRI whole spine. Single-step oncology emergency recall. Distractors A (x-rays + outpatient MRI 1 wk - wildly wrong), C (LP - wrong test), D (analgesia + clinic - dangerous), E (bone scan - wrong). All caricatures. DISCARD.

Malignancy-related hypercalcaemia -> aggressive IV fluids then bisphosphonate. Single-step recall. Distractor B (frusemide before fluid - outdated dogma but rarely chosen), C (calcitonin alone), D (prednisolone alone), E (HD first-line) all caricatures. DISCARD.

Massive transfusion + ionised Ca 0.84 + K 6.2 + ECG changes -> Ca gluconate first (treats both hypocalcaemia AND stabilises myocardium for hyperK). Tests the dual-rationale priority. Distractor B (insulin/dex shift - good for K but doesn't fix Ca), C (HCO3), D (rewarming), E (stop MTP) are real competing priorities. Multi-step.

Septic urosepsis + AKI -> gent + ampi/amox per eTG empirical adult sepsis (urinary source). Distractor B (ceftriax+metro - bowel-source not urinary), D (pip-taz - over-broad), E (mero - over-broad). Multi-step.

Septic shock unresponsive to 30mL/kg + MAP 63 + UO 12 -> noradrenaline. Surviving Sepsis MAP 65 target. Distractor A (more fluid - inappropriate given fluid-refractory), C (steroid without pressor), D (escalate abx), E (transfuse). Multi-step.

Bacterial meningitis suspected + age + immunosuppressed -> dex with/before ceftriax + benzylpen (Listeria cover). Distractor A (no Listeria), B (vanc+mero without dex, no Listeria cover via mero), D (no Listeria, +aciclovir as if encephalitis), E (delay - unsafe). Multi-step.

Encephalitis features (temporal-lobe dysphasia, seizure, behavioural change) -> empirical IV aciclovir without waiting for LP. Tests time-to-aciclovir as priority. Distractor B (wait for PCR) is real-world delay trap. Multi-step.

Acute HIV seroconversion (window between Ag/Ab+ and viral load detection) -> 4th-gen Ag/Ab + RNA viral load. Distractor A (Ab-only rapid - misses window), C (Western blot screening - outdated), D (CD4), E (delay 12 wk - real-world bad practice). Multi-step.

CD4 134 + toxo IgG+ -> bactrim DS for both PJP and toxo (one drug, two indications). Tests prophylaxis CD4 thresholds + dual-coverage knowledge. Distractor A (azithro for MAC - threshold CD4<50), C (CMV proph - not standard), D (itraconazole - wrong context), E (INH - latent TB already excluded). Multi-step.

BUG: option B and option C have identical text (both say tenofovir-emtricitabine without third agent). This means the question has TWO identical 'correct' options or one is a typo. Standard ASHM PEP for high-risk receptive vaginal sex with HIV+ source (regardless of U=U claim if unconfirmed) is 3-drug PEP (TDF/FTC + raltegravir or dolutegravir), not PrEP and not 2-drug. The CORRECT answer should be 3-drug PEP, not PrEP. Major error - flag for rewrite with proper PEP regimen as correct.

Latent TB + nursing student + IGRA+ -> rifampicin 4 months (or INH 6-9 months per TSANZ). Tests indication for LTBI treatment and IGRA preference over TST in BCG-vaccinated. Distractor B (Mantoux to confirm - wrong, IGRA is more specific in BCG-vacc), C (active TB - wrong CXR), D (no treatment with surveillance - inadequate), E (repeat IGRA - not the pathway). Multi-step.

Severe falciparum malaria (8% parasitaemia + AKI + hypoglyc + jaundice + GCS 13) -> IV artesunate. Single-step recognition. Distractors B (oral artemether-lumefantrine - mild only), C (chloroquine - wrong for falciparum), D (quinine - second-line if no artesunate), E (doxycycline mono - wrong) all caricatures. DISCARD.

Necrotising fasciitis - pain out of proportion + bullae + fascial gas. Correct bundle: surgery + mero + clinda (toxin suppression). Lead-in 'most appropriate next step' singular. Distractors B/C/D/E (mono-fluclox, oral, HBO alone, MRI before surgery) caricatures. Tighten lead-in to 'most appropriate combined management' or split.

BUG/ERROR: COVID-19 day 6 + hypoxic + hospitalised - the CORRECT therapy per RECOVERY/Aus COVID-19 Living Guidelines is DEXAMETHASONE, not nirmatrelvir. Nirmatrelvir is for early outpatient treatment in mild disease within 5 days of symptom onset, NOT for hypoxic hospitalised patients. The marked-correct answer (B) is medically WRONG; option A (dexamethasone) is the correct answer for this scenario. Major content error - DISCARD or rewrite stem to be early outpatient mild disease (where nirmatrelvir IS first-line).

Influenza in hospitalised elderly + comorbid -> oseltamivir, renal-dose-reduced. Tests recognition that >48h symptom onset still warrants antiviral in severe/hospitalised AND renal dose adjustment. Distractor D (no antiviral because >48h - real trap), B (standard dose without adjustment), C (baloxavir not first-line for hosp), E (zanamivir wrong route+dose). Multi-step.

Pyelonephritis requiring admission -> IV gent + amoxicillin per eTG. Distractors A (mero too broad), B (nitrofurantoin - inadequate tissue penetration for upper UTI), C (trim 14d outpatient - inadequate for admission), E (cipro outpatient - inadequate disposition). All non-credible because the stem already establishes admission. Replace 2 distractors: 'IV ceftriaxone 1g daily' (real alternative but eTG prefers gent+ampi/amox for empirical) and 'IV pip-taz' (over-broad first-line).

BUG: option A and E are IDENTICAL ('Oral methotrexate 10-15 mg weekly plus folic acid 5 mg weekly') but only E is marked correct. Also tests single-step 'first-line DMARD for RA = MTX' recall. Distractor A (no abx ever - wrong if risk factors emerge), B (start immediately - over-treats), C (swab in labour + delayed treatment - PCR sometimes used but most AU units use risk-factor), E (cefazolin - not first-line). Multi-step.

IOL Bishop 2 + no contraindication -> dinoprostone 2mg gel. Single-step regimen recall. Distractors A (3mg gel - dose wrong for posterior fornix), C (miso 25mcg - acceptable alternative), D (Foley - acceptable alternative), E (oxytocin without ripening) caricatures. DISCARD.

GDM + failed lifestyle for fasting glucose -> metformin first-line per ADIPS. Tests AU first-line oral agent (vs insulin first or US-style glyburide). Distractor B (insulin first - reasonable but not first-line in AU), C (continue lifestyle - inadequate after 3 wk failure), D (glyburide - not AU), E (metformin + insulin together) traps. Multi-step.
Endometritis post-CS -> clinda + gent (or ampi + gent + metro per eTG variant). Single-step regimen recall (similar to obgyn-postnatal-002). DISCARD.
HPV-16 + pLSIL -> colposcopy regardless of cytology grade. Single-step NCSP recall (similar to many obgyn-gynonc/006 etc). DISCARD.

Pre-eclampsia at 36+6 + controlled BP + no severe features -> IOL at 37+0. Tests SOMANZ delivery timing thresholds for non-severe pre-eclampsia. Distractor B (expectant to 39 - false for PET), C (immediate CS - PCR 45 alone is not severe-features), D (delivery 40+0 - delays in PET), E (await steroids effect at 36+6 - too late). Multi-step.

Secondary PPH + endometritis + retained products -> USS + IV abx + oxytocin + theatre for evac. Bundle 4 actions. Lead-in 'next step' singular but congruent. Distractor A (abx + uterotonic without USS - misses retained products), B (digital removal at bedside - inadequate dx), D (uterotonic + observe - dangerous), E (oral + outpt - dangerous).

GDM + failed metformin max + fasting still >5.1 -> add basal insulin (continue metformin). Bundle 2 actions. Lead-in 'next step' singular. Distractor B (cease metformin + basal-bolus - over-treats), C (>2g metformin), D (glyburide), E (only prandial - wrong target). Multi-step.

Term breech -> ECV at 37 wk then CS 39 wk if unsuccessful. Bundle 2 conditional steps. Lead-in 'recommendation' singular. Distractor B (vaginal breech - higher risk per Term Breech Trial), C (CS without ECV offer - removes option), D (ECV without tocolysis - lower success), E (await spontaneous version - 8% chance after 37 wk).

33+5 + APH + planned CS at 36-37 -> ACS course now. Tests recognition of ACS window (up to 34+6 standard, late preterm 34-36+6 with high risk). Distractor B (no ACS beyond 34 - wrong, ACS Late Preterm Trial supports up to 36+6), C (single dose), D (weekly), E (dex superior - false). Multi-step.
Endometritis + sepsis -> admit + IV ampi+gent+metro. Single-step. Same content as several other questions. DISCARD.

Severe PET at 29+3 + stable on Rx -> complete steroid course + deliver at 48h. Tests recognition that even severe PET at preterm gestation may allow brief steroid completion if BP/maternal status controllable. Distractor B (delivery <24h regardless - over-aggressive), C (expectant to 34 - dangerous), E (HELLP criteria not yet met - plt 130 > 100, ALT 110 < usually required threshold for HELLP). Multi-step.

Complete mole -> contraception + monthly hCG surveillance for 6 months after normalisation. Tests recall of surveillance protocol. Distractor A (no surveillance - dangerous), C (hysterectomy mandatory - false), D (MTX prophylaxis - reserved for high-risk GTN), E (CT staging - only with persistent hCG).

Periviable preterm + active resus planned -> give ACS (RANZCOG supports 22+0-22+6 with active resus). Tests recognition of periviability decision. Distractor B (withhold below 23 - older threshold), C (single dose), D (dex 4 doses), E (defer to ROM).

RPL workup: APL antibodies + pelvic USS for uterine anatomy. Bundle 3 tests + USS. Distractor B (adds parental karyotype + thrombophilia - both have lower-evidence recommendations but RANZCOG now suggests targeted), C (general health screen - irrelevant), D (ovarian reserve - irrelevant for RPL), E (MTHFR - low evidence). Acceptable but the correct option excludes parental karyotype, which is debatable in RANZCOG guidance.
VBAC + sudden pain + bleeding + bradycardia = uterine rupture. Single-step pattern recognition. Distractors all clearly distinguishable. DISCARD.

Post-GDM screening at 6-12 wk -> 75g OGTT per ADIPS. Single-step recall. Distractors B (FBG alone - inadequate sens), C (HbA1c - reasonable alt but OGTT preferred), D (no test - dangerous), E (home monitoring) caricatures. DISCARD.

Primary dysmenorrhoea in adolescent -> first-line NSAID (no need for laparoscopy first). Tests recognition that empirical NSAID precedes diagnostic laparoscopy in adolescent dysmenorrhoea. Distractor B (lap first - older paradigm), C (codeine), D (GnRH agonist), E (TENS as monotherapy).

Endometriosis gold standard dx = diagnostic laparoscopy. Single-step recall. Distractors B (TVUS - misses peritoneal disease), C (MRI - reserved for deep infiltrating), D (CA-125 - not diagnostic), E (empiric GnRH as dx test) caricatures. DISCARD.

Pelvic girdle pain in pregnancy -> physio + support belt + analgesia. Tests recognition of PGP. Distractor B (IOL 37 - inappropriate), C (bedrest - worsens), D (corticosteroid inj - last-line), E (opioids - avoid in pregnancy). Multi-step.

Fibroid red degeneration in pregnancy -> conservative analgesia (avoid NSAIDs >32wk for DA constriction). Tests recognition of fibroid red degeneration + AVOID indomethacin at this gestation. Distractor B (indomethacin tds 7d - dangerous), C (myomectomy in pregnancy - contraindicated), D (abruption misdx), E (presumed sepsis without features). Multi-step.

Recurrent 2nd-trimester loss with painless dilatation = cervical insufficiency -> history-indicated cerclage at 12-14 wk. Tests recognition of pattern + intervention. Distractor B (US-indicated only - inadequate for history-positive), C (vag prog only - inadequate), D (bedrest), E (await rescue cerclage). Multi-step.

IUFD at 36+2 + stable -> counsel re DIC risk + IOL. Tests recognition of DIC risk after >48h retention + shared decision-making. Distractor B (CS), C (await spontaneous - DIC risk), D (steroids - dead baby), E (repeat USS - unnecessary delay).

Major abruption + DIC + foetal bradycardia + maternal shock -> MTP + Cat 1 CS. Bundled but congruent with emergency. Distractor B (tocolysis - contraindicated in abruption), C (fluid bolus + reassess - delays delivery), D (ARM for vaginal - too slow), E (transfer - inappropriate). Multi-step.

Late decels on oxytocin + tachysystole 5/10 -> stop oxytocin + LL + terbutaline. Bundle 3 actions. Lead-in 'next step' singular but congruent. Distractor B (continue + deliver - dangerous), C (CS without IUR), D (FSE + FBS - reasonable but second-line), E (O2 + increase oxytocin).

Postnatal depression EPDS 14 + 6 wk postpartum + BF -> psychological therapy + consider sertraline. Bundle 2 actions. Lead-in 'initial management' singular. Distractor B (baby blues - too late at 6 wk), C (admit - no risk evident), D (fluoxetine + stop BF - both incorrect), E (lifestyle alone - inadequate).

Inevitable miscarriage at 8+2 + falling hCG + stable -> offer all 3 management options (expectant/medical/surgical). Tests recognition of patient-led choice. Distractor B (proceed to surgery - removes choice), C (MTX - wrong context), D (oxytocin - not used at this gestation), E (no follow-up - inadequate). Multi-step.
PPROM <34 wk -> oral erythromycin 10d (ORACLE I). Single-step regimen recall. Distractors B/C/D/E all caricatures. DISCARD.

Shoulder dystocia after all internal manoeuvres failed -> Gaskin all-fours + reattempt post arm (ALSO step 4). Tests sequential ladder + AVOID fundal pressure. Distractor B (fundal - contraindicated), C (symphysiotomy - last resort), D (Zavanelli - last resort), E (repeat M+SPP - already tried). Multi-step.

Obstetric APS + already on warfarin (inappropriate for prevention without prior VTE) + new pregnancy -> stop warfarin + ASA + prophylactic LMWH. Bundled but congruent. Tests warfarin teratogenicity + standard APS pregnancy regimen + recognition that prior prescription was wrong. Multi-step.

Maternal sepsis from chorioamnionitis + septic shock -> IV abx within 1h + 30mL/kg fluid + delivery. Bundled correct (3 actions). Lead-in 'next step' singular but Surviving Sepsis 1-hour bundle. Distractor B (ICU first - delays abx), C (CS first - misses sepsis bundle), D (paracetamol - inadequate), E (CT before abx).

Classical scar + TOLAC + rupture features (head receded, fundus not palpable) -> Cat 1 CS + MTP. Single-step pattern + action. Distractors B/C (oxytocin/fundal pressure - dangerous), D (terbutaline - delays), E (workup - delays) all clearly excluded. DISCARD.

Severe HG + before dextrose -> IV thiamine to prevent Wernicke. Single-step recall (same content as obgyn-anscreen-012 thiamine pearl). Distractors B (folate), C (vit K), D (B12), E (no vitamin) caricatures. DISCARD.

Mid-trimester cervical insufficiency with bulging membranes -> emergency cerclage + tocolysis + abx + ACS when feasible. Bundled correct. Lead-in 'combination' plural OK. Tests recognition that cerclage CAN be done with bulging membranes (rescue cerclage). Distractor B (contraindicated - false), C (no indomethacin), D (induce - against patient wishes), E (abdominal cerclage today - reserved for transvaginal failure). Multi-step.

Placenta accreta confirmed + 4 prior CS -> planned CS hysterectomy 34-35+6 + MDT at tertiary + cell salvage + midline incision + MTP standby. Bundled but congruent with major intervention. Tests MDT timing + technique. Multi-step.
